subject: Careers
topic: Bias Mini-Unit — Day 4: Decision Biases and Smarter Choices

worksheet.title: Bias Mini-Unit — Day 4: Decision Biases and Smarter Choices
anchor: Learning goal: I can identify sunk cost fallacy, bandwagon effect, and hindsight bias, and I can use them to improve my own decisions.
Use this worksheet during the mini-lesson, then move into work time on your career presentation.

questions.q_text: Quick start: Why do people sometimes stick with a weak plan even when they know it is not working?
Key notes: Sunk cost fallacy means keeping a weak choice because you already spent time, effort, or money on it. Write your own example or note.
Key notes: Bandwagon effect means believing or choosing something because many other people do. Write your own example or note.
Key notes: Hindsight bias means after something happens, acting like the result was obvious all along. Write your own example or note.

Practice: A student refuses to change a weak topic because they already spent time on it. Bias: ____________________
A career is chosen mainly because it is trendy or popular online. Bias: ____________________
After a strong result, someone says they knew it would work all along. Bias: ____________________
Apply it to your career presentation: What is one part of your presentation you should revise instead of defending?
Apply it to your career presentation: How could popularity affect career choices in unfair ways?
Apply it to your career presentation: What is one thing you improved over time rather than 'always knowing' it would work?
Exit reflection: Choose one bias from the whole mini-unit that will help you most this week. Explain why.

self_check: I can name sunk cost fallacy, bandwagon effect, and hindsight bias.
I connected at least one decision bias to my own presentation work.
I am ready to continue revising or building my presentation.
